description_long: This is a single-story bungalow situated on a slightly irregular grassy plot, featuring a long, low profile and a modern, functional design. The structure has a prominent, dark gray gable roof with multiple intersecting sections, providing visual interest and effective water drainage. The front facade includes a covered entryway and a garage, both sheltered by the extended roof, with paved walkways leading up to them. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely stucco or painted concrete, and are punctuated by a series of rectangular windows, especially along the longer side. The rear and side elevations show a more private aspect, with fewer windows and a clear separation between living and utility spaces. The overall layout and materials suggest a contemporary ranch-style bungalow designed for suburban or semi-urban neighborhoods.
description: A modern, single-story bungalow with a dark gable roof, light exterior walls, and a covered entry and garage, set on a grassy plot. The design features multiple intersecting rooflines and a mix of window arrangements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, garage, and paved walkway, with the gable roof and light-colored facade clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated roofline, the rear garage section, and the irregular shape of the grassy plot.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a long, mostly windowless wall with a row of small windows and the garage area to the left.
description_view_3: Opposite end view showing the covered entryway, the end of the gable roof, and the transition between the main living area and garage.
